Description: Unique identifier for each inventory adjustment transaction
Reference to the specific product being adjusted
Reference to the warehouse location where the adjustment occurred
The date and time when the adjustment was recorded
The numerical change in stock level; negative values indicate stock removal, while positive values indicate stock additions
A short category code for the adjustment reason (e.g., STL for Steal/Shrinkage, EXP for Expired, DAM for Damaged, COR for Correction)
The name of the employee or system user who performed the adjustment
A descriptive explanation of the adjustment reason code
Unique identifier for each product category
Reference to the parent category ID, establishing a hierarchy
The short display name of the category (e.g., 'Printers', 'Furniture')
A detailed description of the types of products included in the category
The depth level of the category in the hierarchy (1 for top-level, 2 for sub-categories)
The display or sort order of the category within its hierarchy level
Unique identifier for each inventory count record
Identifier for the warehouse where the physical count was conducted
The date the physical inventory count was performed
Identifier for the specific product being audited
The theoretical stock quantity expected by the system at the time of the count
The actual physical quantity counted by the staff member
The numerical difference (variance) between the actual and expected quantities
The name of the employee or system user who performed the count
The unique primary identifier for each purchase order.
Foreign key referencing the supplier (inv.sup) from whom the goods are being ordered.
The date the purchase order was created or issued.
The expected delivery date or expiration date for the purchase order.
The current lifecycle status of the purchase order (e.g., 'A' for Approved, 'P' for Pending, 'R' for Received, 'S' for Shipped, 'X' for Cancelled).
The total monetary value of the purchase order, excluding or including shipping depending on business logic.
The cost associated with shipping the order; appears to use a flat rate (150) or zero.
Optional free-text field for special instructions or comments regarding the order.
Foreign key identifying the parent purchase order header in inv.po.
The sequence or line-item number for the product within a specific purchase order.
Foreign key identifying the specific product being ordered, referencing the product master.
The quantity of the product originally requested or ordered from the supplier.
The unit price or cost of the product for this specific purchase order line.
The actual quantity of the product that has been delivered and processed into inventory.
Unique internal identifier for each product record
Reference to the product category (e.g., Furniture, Electronics)
Reference to the primary supplier or vendor for the product
Stock Keeping Unit; a unique alphanumeric code used for inventory tracking
The display name of the product
A brief text description of the product's features or specifications
The retail or selling price of the product
The wholesale cost or acquisition price paid to the supplier
The current lifecycle status of the product (e.g., Active, Discontinued, Obsolete)
The physical weight of the product, likely in pounds or kilograms
Unit of Measure; defines how the product is packaged or sold
Unique identifier for each receiving transaction record
Reference to the purchase order associated with the incoming shipment
The date the goods were physically received at the facility
The specific warehouse or distribution center where the goods were delivered
Internal comments regarding the shipment status, such as discrepancies or backorder information
Foreign key identifying the specific product being tracked.
Foreign key identifying the physical warehouse or distribution center where the stock is located.
The current physical quantity of the product available at this specific warehouse.
The quantity of stock currently reserved or committed to open sales orders but not yet shipped.
The minimum stock threshold or safety stock level used to trigger reordering processes.
The maximum capacity or target stock level for this product at this location.
The date of the last physical inventory count or audit performed for this item at this location.
The date when stock for this product was last received into this warehouse from a supplier or transfer.
Unique identifier for each supplier
The legal or trade name of the supplier company
The current operational status of the supplier account (e.g., Active, Terminated, Suspended, Inactive)
Standard payment terms agreed upon with the supplier
A numerical performance or quality rating assigned to the supplier
The name of the primary contact person at the supplier company
The phone number for the primary contact person
The email address for the primary contact person
Unique surrogate primary key for each warehouse facility
Short mnemonic code for the warehouse, typically using 3-letter airport codes
The full descriptive name of the warehouse facility
The city where the warehouse is physically located
The two-character state abbreviation for the warehouse location
Classification code for the warehouse type (e.g., Regional, Main, Distribution)
The storage capacity of the warehouse, likely measured in square feet or units
The current operational status of the warehouse (e.g., Active, Maintenance)
The unique primary identifier for each address record.
Foreign key linking the address to a specific customer in the sales.cst table.
The category or purpose of the address (e.g., Shipping, Billing, Office).
The primary street address line.
Secondary address information such as suite, apartment, or unit numbers.
The city where the address is located.
The two-character state abbreviation.
The postal or ZIP code.
The country code for the address.
A boolean flag indicating if this is the primary/default address for the customer.
Unique identifier for each customer record
The full name of the customer or business entity
The current status of the customer account (e.g., Active, Suspended, Inactive, Terminated)
The date the customer record was created or the account was opened
The acquisition source or lead channel for the customer (e.g., Website, Store, Referral, Phone)
The identifier of the sales representative or account manager assigned to the customer
Market segment classification (e.g., Wholesale, Retail, Enterprise)
Internal customer rating or tier (1 to 5 scale)
The current outstanding balance on the customer's account
The maximum credit limit allowed for the customer
The date of the most recent order placed by the customer
A unique surrogate identifier for each note entry.
The identifier for the customer associated with the note, linking to sales.cst.
The date the note was created or the interaction occurred.
The name of the staff member or system user who recorded the note.
The detailed content of the note, describing the nature of the customer interaction (e.g., bulk pricing discussions, payment follow-ups).
A category code for the interaction type: M (Meeting), E (Email), O (Order/Other), and C (Correspondence/Collections).
Primary key for the email record
Foreign key referencing the customer who owns this email address
The category or type of email address (e.g., Work, Personal, Other)
The actual email address string
Indicates whether the email address has been verified (e.g., via a confirmation link)
Indicates if this is the primary or default email address for the customer
The unique primary identifier for each individual line item in an order.
The identifier of the parent sales order this line item belongs to.
The identifier of the specific product or stock item being purchased.
The quantity of the product ordered in this specific line item.
The unit price or gross price for the line item before discounts and taxes.
The discount amount applied to this specific line item.
The calculated tax amount for this specific line item.
The sequence or line number of the item within the specific order (e.g., Item 1, Item 2).
The unique primary identifier for each sales order.
Foreign key referencing the customer who placed the order.
The date the order was originally placed by the customer.
The date the order was dispatched to the customer. Contains nulls for orders not yet shipped or cancelled.
The current status of the order (e.g., P=Pending, S=Shipped, D=Delivered, C=Complete, X=Cancelled).
The total monetary value of the order.
The amount of tax calculated for the order.
The shipping fee charged to the customer (e.g., 0 for free, 15 or 25 for paid tiers).
The percentage discount applied to the order total.
The agreed-upon payment terms for the order (e.g., COD=Cash on Delivery, N30=Net 30 days).
Internal or customer-facing notes regarding the order, often used for special handling instructions.
The unique primary identifier for each phone record.
The identifier of the customer to whom the phone number belongs.
The category or type of phone number (e.g., W=Work, M=Mobile, H=Home, F=Fax).
The actual phone number string, stored in various formats.
The telephone extension, typically used for work or office numbers.
A boolean flag indicating if this is the primary or default phone number for the customer.
The unique primary identifier for each payment transaction record.
A reference to the specific sales order that this payment is intended to satisfy. The 10% null rate suggests that some payments are unallocated or represent general account credits.
The identifier of the customer who made the payment.
The date on which the payment was received or processed.
The monetary value of the payment transaction.
The medium used for the payment. Codes likely represent: CC (Credit Card), CA (Cash), CK (Check), and WR (Wire Transfer).
The current processing state of the payment. Likely codes: A (Applied/Approved), F (Failed), P (Pending), R (Refunded/Rejected).
A unique reference string or transaction number, likely used for auditing or external reconciliation (e.g., bank transaction ID).
Supplemental information about the payment, specifically used to flag transactions that are applied as account credits rather than direct order payments.
The unique primary identifier for each return record.
Foreign key referencing the original sales order associated with the return.
Foreign key referencing the specific line item within an order that is being returned.
The date the return was initiated or processed.
A short code representing the reason for the return (e.g., WRG, DOA, DMG, CHG).
The number of units of the specific line item being returned.
The monetary value or refund amount associated with the return.
The current processing status of the return (e.g., Approved, Rejected, Pending, Completed).
A text description explaining the reason for the return in detail.
The unique primary identifier for a shipment record.
The identifier of the sales order being fulfilled by this shipment.
The identifier of the warehouse from which the goods were dispatched.
The date the shipment left the warehouse.
The date the shipment was confirmed as delivered to the customer.
The logistics provider or service level used for the shipment (e.g., FedEx, UPS, DHL, USPS).
The unique tracking number provided by the carrier for shipment monitoring.
The current status of the shipment (e.g., 'D' for Delivered, 'S' for Shipped, 'P' for Pending, 'N' for New).
The physical weight of the shipment, likely used for calculating shipping costs.
The monetary cost incurred for the shipping service.
